introduction: I’m an incoming Ph.D. student in Computer Science at the University of British Columbia. I currently work as a Research Assistant in National Taiwan University EECS department under Prof. Yu-Chiang Frank Wang. Before that, I was a visiting student with Prof. Ming-Hsuan Yang at the University of California, Merced.
My Primary research interests lie in Computer Vision, with a particular focus on multi-modal models' adaptability, continual learning, and open-domain 3D articulation synthesis.
Prior to my role at VLLab, I am a Machine Learning Engineer at Dcard, where I designed a recommendation system using machine learning algorithms for over 15 million users.
I completed my bachelor's and master's degrees in Computer Science from National Taiwan University, where I conducted research in the Computational Learning Lab Prof. Hsuan-Tien Lin.
